title: Crispy Air Fryer Brussels Sprouts with Parmesan: A Game-Changer!
promptIMG: Food photography: Crispy Air Fryer Brussels Sprouts with Parmesan, appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2
description: These crispy Brussels sprouts are a delightful side dish that’s ready in just 20 minutes! Tossed with Parmesan for a savory crunch, they’re perfect for weeknight dinners or holiday feasts.
ingredients: 1 pound Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved
instructions: In a large bowl, toss the halved Brussels sprouts with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Place the Brussels sprouts in the air fryer basket in a single layer. Cook for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, shake the basket to ensure even cooking, then sprinkle the Parmesan cheese over the sprouts.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the sprouts are crispy and the cheese is golden.
Remove from the air fryer and serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan if desired.
tips: For extra flavor, try adding a squeeze of lemon juice before serving. A common mistake is overcrowding the air fryer; make sure the sprouts are in a single layer for maximum crispiness. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days, but they’re best enjoyed fresh!

structured_content.content: I absolutely love these crispy air fryer Brussels sprouts! They're not just a fantastic side dish; they’re a game-changer for anyone who thinks they don’t like Brussels sprouts. The air fryer makes them perfectly crispy without all the oil of traditional frying. Plus, the addition of Parmesan cheese takes them to a whole new level. I usually whip these up for family dinners or when I have friends over, and they disappear in no time!

Here's the thing - You'll need fresh Brussels sprouts, which are key for that crunch, and good quality Parmesan cheese for that savory flavor. Olive oil helps them crisp up beautifully. If you don't have garlic powder, fresh minced garlic works too, just be careful not to burn it.

Here's the thing - The process is super simple. Just toss the sprouts with olive oil and seasonings, air fry them until they're golden, then add the cheese for the last few minutes. You’ll love how quick it is!

upload.context: Crispy Air Fryer Brussels Sprouts with Parmesan on a white plate

structured_content.content: These Brussels sprouts are fantastic on their own, but they also pair beautifully with roasted meats or as part of a holiday spread. You can even toss them in a salad for a crunchy texture. Feel free to experiment with different seasonings—maybe some chili flakes for heat or balsamic glaze for sweetness!
